---
title: "Population Grids"
subtitle: "Comparing census-derived population against gridded population products"
toc: false
---

This site documents the research and the reproducible data pipeline behind a
paper comparing **census-derived population counts** against **gridded population
products** (e.g. GHSL `GHS_POP`), and surveys where the underlying census data is
openly available worldwide.

## The pipeline

A catalog-driven downloader (`popgrids`, run via the **`download-census`** CLI)
acquires the finest official census population per country into standardized
**GeoParquet**, across regions:

```bash
download-census --list                     # coverage table + data-quality scores
download-census --countries DE US BR       # specific countries
download-census --region europe            # a whole region
download-census --layers quality           # World Bank SPI data-quality table
```

Outputs land in `data/<region>/<country>/…` (gitignored). Each country is one
catalog entry; adding a country is a catalog entry plus an existing adapter.

### Coverage at a glance

- **17 datasets verified** end-to-end against national totals across **Europe**
  (DE, NL, FR×2, IT, ES, UK, IE, PT, CH, NO, PL, SE), the **Americas**
  (US blocks, Canada dissemination blocks, Brazil setores), and
  **Asia-Pacific** (Australia mesh blocks).
- The **Eurostat GEOSTAT 1 km** grid as a homogenized European baseline, and the
  global **GHS-UCDB** urban-centre reference.
- A **per-country data-quality** column (World Bank SPI) on the coverage list.

## Read more

- [Europe — sources, workflow & current data](docs/europe_census_data.md)
- [Global census-data availability survey](docs/global_census_availability.md)
- [Time-series feasibility](docs/time_series_feasibility.md)

## Notebooks

- [01 · Generate Earth Engine assets](notebooks/01_generate_assets.ipynb) —
  promotes census geometry into Earth Engine.
- [Census vs GHSL differences](notebooks/differences.ipynb) — sums GHSL raster
  population within census polygons for the comparison.
